ending: Omega escapes the Ornu shipyard with a newly installed short-range jump drive after destroying Pertinax's flagship. The drive causes Omega pain and structural strain, so its use is limited, and Pertinax survives by fleeing in a small craft. Bill remains captain and king within Omega's governing framework. Bina, Alden Stone, Hans Norder, Felicia Keating, James Riding, and the surviving core Marines remain aboard. Funnybone, Splat, and Termite are alive in medical care, while Sally Longfield died during the earlier coup. Riding has put aside his remaining resentment toward Bina, although Keating's distrust is unresolved. The human-Roughback alliance remains in force, but Tull and the last Roughback crew refuse Bill's offer to return and defend their world. They give humanity their remaining military support so Omega can seek a home and eventually oppose the Imperium. Omega sets course for a habitable, resource-rich settlement world. The Ornu pursuit remains active, and an ancient Konami rival reaches Philo's imprisoned nephew, reveals knowledge of Omega's destination, and forms an alliance with him to destroy the ship.
in_short: After Earth is lost, Bill Henderson commands Omega and its roughly two million refugees through famine, a Slovenian militia coup, and repeated Imperial attacks. Bina Chakravarti reaches into Omega's ancient Konami past and secures food-producing nourishers, while Omega restores contact through Val and requires Bill to rule the refugees as king. The crew defeats the coup, publicly confronts Bina's betrayal of James Riding, and wins a formal alliance with the Roughbacks after surviving a combined fleet and ground invasion. Omega then seizes an Ornu shipyard for a short-range jump drive. During the siege, Splat captures an enemy warship, the Roughbacks suffer devastating losses, and the completed drive lets Omega destroy Pertinax's flagship and rescue its surviving ground force. Pertinax escapes. Tull sends humanity onward to an Omega-selected settlement world while the Roughbacks remain behind. An ancient Konami rival knows that destination and recruits Philo's imprisoned nephew to help destroy Omega.

recaps.text: Previously: Earth has been lost, and Omega now carries roughly two million human refugees. Bill Henderson serves as the ancient warship's captain and the accepted leader of the displaced population. Bina Chakravarti is his executive officer, Alden Stone commands the surviving Marines, and Hans Norder, Felicia Keating, Sally Longfield, and the comatose James Riding belong to the command crew. Bill's parents have been recovered from forced labor, but his father Gordon remains badly traumatized. Tull represents the Roughbacks, whose interests overlap with humanity's resistance to the Ornu Imperium. Omega and its adviser Val have previously aided Bill, but both are now unreachable. The ship is overcrowded, its food, water, sanitation, and security are under severe strain, and other human prisoners remain in Imperial camps. The Ornu still regard Omega as a strategic prize. Pertinax is among the commanders pursuing it, while the politically connected consul Philo is tied to an imprisoned former signifier blamed for losing the ship. Bill must preserve the refugee population, restore Omega's guidance, and find a path beyond Imperial control.

Omega's refugee population has exceeded the ship's capacity, leaving food, water, sanitation, and security near failure. Bill cannot reach Omega or Val, so he and his officers decide to seek a short-range jump drive at an Ornu shipyard. They also hope to obtain supplies and Roughback support, although Tull warns that Roughback leaders may try to claim Omega. Meanwhile, Alden Stone's depleted Marine team begins recruiting replacements and civilian peacekeepers. A politically connected Ornu consul, Philo, is linked to an imprisoned nephew who expects to be blamed for the loss of Omega. A supposed rescued prime minister offers Bill a large cache of crowd-control weapons as coordinated groups pressure multiple food stores. Bill accepts the equipment when a supply chamber is threatened. At one barricade, Guns prevents a civilian officer from firing live ammunition into the refugees. Bill, Stone, the Marines, and civilian security disperse the crowd with nonlethal weapons, though one woman suffers a serious injury. The immediate riot ends, but its coordination and the unexplained weapons stockpile suggest that the disorder is being directed.

Deaths and severe decline among recovered forced-labor prisoners reinforce Bill's determination to find a safe destination. Marine recruitment is disrupted when a promising trainee is poisoned with strychnine. She survives, but the investigation identifies neither the poisoner nor a secure group of candidates. Bina visits the comatose James Riding, helps distribute medical supplies, and defends Bill's leadership to the lower decks. A young refugee then reveals a hidden cache of lethal weapons. She explains that a Slovenian militia seized an evacuation shuttle, murdered its intended passengers, smuggled the arsenal aboard Omega, and organized the food riots. When the supposed Slovenian prime minister confronts Bill, Bina signals danger and Sally Longfield identifies him as the militia leader believed dead on Earth. His guards draw concealed firearms. Bill kills the impostor in the ensuing firefight, exposing an armed conspiracy embedded among the refugees. The militia's leader is dead, but his people, weapons, and wider takeover plan remain active aboard the already unstable ship.

The Slovenian militia launches a coordinated takeover aimed at Omega's weapons, engineering spaces, supplies, and bridge. Armed fighters wound a Marine, capture Hans Norder, and trap Alden Stone and Funnybone near the weapons cache. Bill broadcasts the dead impostor's identity, weakening civilian support for the conspiracy, but hunger-driven looting overruns a ration bay. The militia leader's son assumes command, murders the civilian who guided Stone's team, and withdraws when other Marines arrive. Bill, Bina, Sally Longfield, and Felicia Keating fight toward the bridge, but Longfield and Keating disappear after going ahead. Philo, meanwhile, abandons his imprisoned nephew and joins Pertinax in preparing a false treason case against him. The new militia leader confirms that he holds Longfield, Keating, and Norder. He threatens Bill's parents and promises hourly executions unless Bill yields Omega. Bina proposes a tactical surrender to preserve lives and create a later opportunity to strike back. After the militia leader tortures and kills Longfield while Bill listens, Bill formally gives up control of the ship.

The militia occupies Omega's bridge and forces Bill and Norder to operate the ship, while Keating remains a hostage. Bina escapes into the Archive, where a Konami simulation links an ancient civilizational collapse to engineered disease, technological dependence, and the exploitation of void servants. The record leads her to a metal artifact that speaks to its discoverer, but ends before explaining it. On the lower decks, the new regime murders unarmed civilians, coerces the Marines, and orders Stone and Funnybone to train militia personnel beside their own recruits. Tubes is badly wounded during a fight over stolen food, and Stone stops Termite from executing the attacker. The coup leader then sends Bill to negotiate supplies from an Ornu vassal world while using hostages to control Stone on the bridge. Bill wins authorization for food and minerals from the world's regional senators, who also report that the Perseids have invaded the Imperium. A civilian resistance begins organizing aboard Omega around evidence of Bill's conduct. Before that resistance can act, a hostile fleet approaches. Bill recommends withdrawal, but the coup leader demands battle, leaving Bill to defend Omega against incoming long-range missiles.

Bina's Archive experience reveals that Omega spoke to a young Konami revolutionary long before the Primeval civilization. Omega permits Bina one intervention in that past, and she asks the future builder to prioritize food-producing nourishers. An emissary later explains that Omega will assist because Bill has acted honorably, but will not remove human freedom by ruling directly. During the fleet battle, Bill and Stone covertly cooperate while Omega and Val prepare Bill to retake command. Omega blacks out the ship. Stone, the Marines, and civilian allies use infrared vision during the counterattack. Tull's Roughbacks decline to join the internal fight, but carry Stone through the darkness and later defend storage. Funnybone rescues Norder and Keating, while Stone accepts surrender from militia members rather than killing them. The coup leader returns to the bridge intending to assassinate Bill, but fires into an empty command chair. When the lights return, he refuses surrender and reaches for a hidden gun, and Bill kills him. The takeover ends. Omega leaves the system with newly acquired food and minerals while surviving enemy ships pursue. Bina must still bring the nourishers into use, and Bill must restore order, reunite his officers, and decide how to govern the refugee population.

Omega's nourishers begin converting available matter into food, ending the immediate famine. Val tells Bill that the divided refugee population needs a final authority and that Omega requires him to rule as king beneath its own authority. Bill accepts, though Bina urges him to build laws and institutions rather than depend on one ruler. James Riding recovers and publicly accuses Bina of informing on his plan to retaliate against an Ornu official. She admits it, explaining that she feared collective punishment. Bill keeps her in command, but Riding withdraws and Keating rejects her personally. Omega then reaches the devastated Roughback homeworld. Bill leaves Bina in command and travels underground with Tull, Splat, and Funnybone to seek a formal alliance. Tull presents humanity's rebellion to a public Roughback confluence. Bill promises a shared struggle against the Ornu but cannot offer a specific long-term reward. The confluence agrees to consider the alliance only if Bill proves his commitment by laying down his life for Roughback survival.

Tull offers Bill several nearly fatal ways to satisfy the Roughbacks' demand, including execution by a device called the Disseverer. Bill refuses deliberate execution and asks for another path. Before the question is settled, a large enemy fleet attacks Omega and the Roughback world. Tull brings Bill's delegation to a concealed asteroid warship, and the Roughbacks join the defense because their own people are threatened. Bina directs Omega with Norder and Keating while Val assists. Bill initially considers withdrawal, but Bina persuades him to remain beside the new allies. Omega and two Roughback asteroid vessels combine missile fire with guided fragments torn from the Roughback ships. One allied vessel is destroyed, and Omega's ammunition falls dangerously low. Val directs Bill toward a nearby asteroid belt without explaining Omega's full plan. At the same time, enemy troops begin landing to destroy the Roughbacks underground. Stone argues that humanity cannot abandon them. Bill authorizes him to take the Marines, volunteer trainees, and Tull to the surface, turning the ground defense into humanity's practical demonstration of alliance.

Stone and Tull prepare a small mixed force to defend underground exits against enemy soldiers and hired jackals. The Marines use darkness, blocked rail routes, and ambushes to inflict heavy losses, but explosive trains destroy their platform, injure Guns, kill trainees, and trap the survivors below ground. In space, Bill follows Val's unexplained order into an asteroid belt despite impacts, depleted weapons, and near-certain destruction. Bina notices asteroids moving unnaturally. They converge on the pursuing fleet and destroy it, leaving Omega and one Roughback vessel alive, though the mechanism remains unknown. Tull and Roughback rescuers drill through to Stone's trapped team. The Marines identify and capture a traitor who poisoned a teammate to replace her and tried to deliver Bill to the enemy. He confesses, claims he acted alone, and then kills himself with poison before a fuller interrogation. The Roughback confluence formally recognizes humans as kin, creating an unprecedented species-wide alliance without requiring Bill's execution. Tull becomes its ambassador aboard Omega. The victory brings a larger danger: the Ornu and Perseids have aligned against Bill. Omega must now seek more allies and obtain the short-range jump drive.

Bina investigates the asteroid victory and finds that Roughback mining records explain only some of the altered rocks, leaving much of Omega's intervention unresolved. Imperial leaders also react to the defeat. Philo argues that Bill's power comes from Omega, and the Ornu ruler gives Pertinax enemy warships and hired jackals to capture or destroy the ship. In Omega's Archive, Bina sees the Konami project leader plan a vast spacecraft. A powerful, long-lived rival offers exclusive financing while claiming a shared desire to outlast their species. The project leader refuses because the rival enslaves void servants. The rejected rival vows that his own work will surpass and destroy the project. Val then gathers Bill's senior officers in simulation space to plan the short-range jump drive installation. James Riding confronts Bina, but chooses to move forward unless she repeats her betrayal; Keating remains wary. Omega's drones can install the drive in about five hours, provided the crew seizes an Ornu shipyard and protects the operation. The officers commit to the assault, supported by Roughback ships and an expanded ground force equipped with new armor made by Omega.

An unidentified living being escapes from recovered Primeval technology and kills an Ornu research intern. Aboard Omega, a multinational group of former military personnel volunteers for the shipyard attack, though Stone remains cautious because someone has leaked the plan. Omega reaches the only suitable shipyard, disables its immediate defenses, and docks while eight warships approach. Stone leads Marines and volunteers onto the station. Shipyard personnel increase gravity to immobilize the volunteers and delay Omega's drones, but the Marine systems specialist restores reduced gravity and the labor crew surrenders. Installation begins. Tull coordinates the Roughback screen as Pertinax arrives with an Ornu flagship, enemy warships, and jackal gunships. Covert enemy vessels reach the station, destroy its fixed turrets with planted explosives, jam communications, and isolate the ground force. Funnybone's team becomes surrounded inside a docked warship. Pertinax demands surrender and threatens extermination while Roughback defenses collapse. Bill considers giving himself up, but Bina, Riding, and Keating reject the proposal. They believe Imperial mercy offers no path to freedom and choose to keep fighting while the drive remains unfinished.

Splat fights through the docked enemy warship, reaches its bridge, kills the Ornu supervisor, disables the jammer, and takes control. He flies the ship beyond the station's gravity field, expels the jackals from its open cargo bay, and rescues the wounded Funnybone and two recruits. Tull's remaining Roughback ships continue shielding Omega even as their vessels approach structural collapse. On the station, Alden and another Marine free Termite from debris. Termite helps defend the control specialist against a large enemy assault until his ammunition is exhausted. With defeat imminent, Bill brings his parents to witness the battle. Gordon briefly regains clarity and affirms Bill's resistance. Val then reveals that the short-range jump drive is nearly ready. The station vents its atmosphere and cuts gravity while the captured warship gathers survivors. Omega activates the drive, jumps beyond the enemy fleet, and uses successive jumps and weapons fire to destroy the attackers, including Pertinax's flagship. Pertinax escapes in a small craft. Omega recovers the surviving ground force and Roughbacks, then jumps away before reinforcements arrive. The drive works, but it causes Omega pain and structural strain.

After the shipyard escape, the surviving officers, Marines, and Roughback crew assess their losses. Funnybone, Splat, and Termite remain alive in medical care. Bill proposes returning to defend the Roughback homeworld from the Ornu response. Tull and the last Roughback crew refuse. Their confluence chose to face its own fate while humanity uses the alliance's remaining military support to find a home and eventually challenge the Imperium. Bill accepts their decision and directs Omega toward a habitable, resource-rich settlement world selected by the ship. James Riding privately recognizes Bina's change and releases his remaining resentment. The immediate goals of food supply, alliance, and a short-range jump drive have been achieved, but Omega cannot use the new drive without pain and structural strain, and Pertinax remains free. In prison, Philo's former-signifier nephew learns of the Imperial defeat. An unfamiliar bipedal stranger reaches his cell, reveals knowledge of Omega and its destination, and identifies himself as the ancient Konami rival seen in the Archive. The prisoner joins him in a revenge alliance aimed at destroying Bill and Omega.
